title: Companies House Free Company Data Product
short_description: Monthly snapshot of basic data for all live UK companies including registration, status, and SIC codes.
long_description: Monthly bulk CSV snapshot of all active companies on the UK register, including registered address, company type, status, SIC codes, and key dates. Updated within 5 working days of month end. Contains approximately 5 million active companies split across multiple ZIP files. Widely used for business intelligence, compliance screening, and market analysis. Does not include dissolved companies or detailed officer/shareholder data.
date_coverage: Single monthly snapshot updated each month end
schema_fields: CompanyName|string|Registered company name; CompanyNumber|string|Unique 8-digit company identifier; RegAddress.AddressLine1|string|First line of registered address; RegAddress.PostCode|string|Postcode of registered office; CompanyCategory|string|Legal type e.g. Private Limited Company; CompanyStatus|string|Current status e.g. Active/Liquidation; IncorporationDate|date|Date company was incorporated DD/MM/YYYY; SICCode.SicText_1|string|Primary SIC code and description
sample_rows: ANZON LIMITED,00001548,CALDER HOUSE,HG1 2UD,Private Limited Company,Active,12/10/1898,47110 - Retail sale in non-specialised stores; AIREDALE ALLOYS LIMITED,00001567,INGS ROAD,BD21 4DT,Private Limited Company,Active,01/01/1896,24200 - Manufacture of tubes pipes hollow profiles; BRITISH MUSEUM,00104721,GREAT RUSSELL STREET,WC1B 3DG,PRI/LBG/NSC (Private, Limited by guarantee, no share capital),Active,01/01/1963,91020 - Museum activities
tags: companies, register, corporate, UK, incorporation, SIC codes, business, addresses, compliance
notes: ~55 fields total. Field spec PDF at https://resources.companieshouse.gov.uk/toolsToHelp/pdf/freeDataProductDataset.pdf. Companion API provides real-time officer/filing data.

title: HM Land Registry Price Paid Data
short_description: All residential property sales in England and Wales from January 1995 to present with price and full address.
long_description: Complete record of every residential property transaction registered with HMLR since January 1995, covering over 28 million sales. Includes sale price, completion date, full address, property type, freehold/leasehold tenure, and whether new build. Updated monthly. Approximately 1 million new transactions added per year. Does not cover Scotland, Northern Ireland, or commercial property. Foundational for housing market analysis, automated valuations, and economic research.
access_method: Free bulk download — full file 5.3 GB CSV or annual splits
schema_fields: transaction_unique_identifier|string|Auto-generated unique reference; price|integer|Sale price GBP; date_of_transfer|date|Completion date YYYY-MM-DD; postcode|string|Property postcode; property_type|string|D=Detached S=Semi T=Terraced F=Flat O=Other; old_new|string|Y=new build N=established; duration|string|F=Freehold L=Leasehold; street|string|Street name; town_city|string|Town or city; county|string|County name
sample_rows: {B40CF7A6-5099-4153-B545-45F16BB00B7D},95000,1995-06-30,BS5 8JB,T,N,L,8,WYKEN ROAD,BRISTOL,CITY OF BRISTOL,A; {A8B6E5E2-4C68-41C2-9A9D-0B2E7D24F6D3},375000,2010-03-15,SW1A 2AA,F,N,L,45,VICTORIA STREET,LONDON,GREATER LONDON,A; {C7D3F1B9-8E2A-4B6C-A1D5-9F0E8C7D2B4A},620000,2023-07-21,W1K 4HR,F,N,L,12,MAYFAIR PLACE,LONDON,GREATER LONDON,A
tags: property, housing, real estate, sales, prices, land registry, residential, England, Wales, transactions
notes: No column headers in raw file by default. Full file 5.3 GB. Linked data version via SPARQL at landregistry.data.gov.uk. Category Type B includes repossessions and buy-to-lets.

title: Charity Commission Register of Charities
short_description: Full register of all charities in England and Wales including income, classification, trustees, and registration dates.
long_description: Daily extract of all registered and recently removed charities in England and Wales, covering approximately 170,000 organisations across 15 linked tables. Includes registration history, trustee details, area of operation, classification by theme and beneficiary, and annual financials. Income and expenditure data available for charities with income over £500,000. Updated daily. Widely used for due diligence, grant-making, and civil society research.
provider: Charity Commission for England and Wales
date_coverage: All currently and recently registered charities; historical from founding
schema_fields: regno|integer|Unique charity registration number; subno|integer|Subsidiary number; name|string|Registered charity name; orgtype|string|Organisation type code; income|integer|Latest annual income GBP; expenditure|integer|Latest annual expenditure GBP; registration_date|date|Date first registered; removal_date|date|Date removed from register if applicable
tags: charities, nonprofits, third sector, England, Wales, income, trustees, registration, voluntary sector
notes: 15 linked relational tables joined on regno+subno. Detailed financials only for income above £500k. Format changed from BCP to JSON/TSV in 2021.

title: Ofsted State-funded School Inspections and Outcomes
short_description: Monthly dataset of all Ofsted inspection outcomes for state-funded schools in England with judgements across key areas.
long_description: Monthly management information dataset covering all Ofsted inspection outcomes for approximately 22,000 state-funded schools in England including primary, secondary, special schools, and alternative provision. Contains inspection dates, overall effectiveness judgements on a 1-4 scale, and sub-judgements for quality of education, behaviour, personal development, and leadership. Covers September 2005 to present. Key resource for local authority benchmarking and school improvement planning.
provider: Office for Standards in Education Children's Services and Skills (Ofsted)
schema_fields: URN|string|Unique Reference Number; EstablishmentName|string|School name; OverallEffectiveness|integer|Overall Ofsted judgement 1-4; QualityOfEducation|integer|Quality of education sub-judgement; LeadershipAndManagement|integer|Leadership sub-judgement; InspectionDate|date|Date of most recent inspection; SchoolType|string|Type of school; Postcode|string|School postcode
sample_rows: 100000,Riverside Primary School,2,2,2,15/11/2022,Community School,SW1A 2AA; 100001,St Mary Catholic Academy,1,1,1,10/03/2023,Academy Converter,M1 2AB; 100002,Hillside Secondary School,3,3,3,07/09/2023,Foundation School,LS1 4DY
tags: schools, Ofsted, inspection, education, England, outstanding, primary, secondary, academy, judgement
notes: Separate datasets for early years, further education, and children's social care. URN links to GIAS register. Five-Year Ofsted Inspection Data also published annually.

title: ONS Mid-Year Population Estimates England and Wales
short_description: Annual mid-year population estimates for England and Wales by local authority, single year of age, and sex from 1971.
long_description: Annual official population estimates for England and Wales published by ONS, covering all local authorities and London boroughs by single year of age and sex. Includes components of change: births, deaths, and internal and international migration. Available from 1971 to present. Published annually approximately 18 months after the reference year. Used by NHS, local authorities, and central government for resource allocation and demographic analysis.
access_method: Free bulk download; Free API via developer.ons.gov.uk
schema_fields: geography_code|string|ONS geography code e.g. E09000001; geography_name|string|Local authority name; year|integer|Reference mid-year; age|integer|Single year of age 0-90+; sex|string|Male/Female/Persons; population|integer|Estimated mid-year population count
sample_rows: E09000001,City of London,2023,25,Male,1450; E09000002,Barking and Dagenham,2023,0,Persons,4234; E92000001,England,2023,45,Female,2143800
tags: population, demographics, local authority, London, age, sex, migration, births, deaths, England, Wales
notes: ONS API at developer.ons.gov.uk. 2021 Census-based estimates rebased from 2022 release. Ethnicity breakdowns published separately.

title: UK House Price Index
short_description: Monthly house price index for 441 UK areas from 1995 broken down by property type and buyer status.
long_description: Official UK HPI published jointly by HMLR, ONS, Registers of Scotland, and NISRA. Covers average prices and index values (base 100 = January 2023) for 441 geographic areas including regions, counties, local authorities, and London boroughs. Includes breakdowns by property type and buyer type (first-time buyer, existing owner). Published monthly with approximately 2-month lag.
date_coverage: January 1995 to present (England and Wales); 2004 Scotland; 2005 Northern Ireland
access_method: Free bulk download; Free API (SPARQL endpoint)
schema_fields: refArea|string|ONS geography code; refPeriod|date|Reference month YYYY-MM-DD; averagePrice|decimal|Mean house price GBP; index|decimal|Index value; percentageChange|decimal|Monthly % change; percentageAnnualChange|decimal|Annual % change; salesVolume|integer|Number of sales in period
tags: house prices, property, index, housing market, UK, London, mortgage, real estate, first time buyer
notes: SPARQL endpoint at landregistry.data.gov.uk/landregistry/query. Sales volume has 3-4 month additional lag. Re-referenced to January 2023 from February 2025.

title: FCA Financial Services Register
short_description: Live register of all FCA-authorised firms and individuals in UK financial services including permissions and regulatory status.
long_description: The Financial Services Register is the definitive record of all firms and individuals authorised or registered by the FCA, covering approximately 50,000 authorised firms and 1.5 million approved individuals. Includes firm permissions, trading names, appointed representatives, and current regulatory status. Updated in real-time. Public API free with registration; bulk extract requires paid subscription via Register Extract Service (RES).
licence: UNKNOWN — check publisher terms (public search free; bulk extract via commercial Register Extract Service)
format: REST API; CSV bulk extract via paid RES
access_method: Free API — registration required; Paid subscription for bulk extract
schema_fields: firm_reference_number|string|Unique FCA firm reference number; firm_name|string|Registered firm name; status|string|Authorised/Registered/Appointed Representative; permissions|string|List of regulated activities; registered_address|string|Principal place of business; date_authorised|date|Date of FCA authorisation; firm_type|string|Type e.g. Bank/Insurer/IFA
sample_rows: 122702,BARCLAYS BANK PLC,Authorised,Accepting deposits; Issuing electronic money,1 Churchill Place London E14 5HP,1997-01-01,Bank; 106054,AVIVA INSURANCE LIMITED,Authorised,Effecting contracts of insurance,St Helen's 1 Undershaft London EC3P 3DQ,1994-09-01,Insurer
tags: FCA, financial services, authorised firms, regulated, compliance, banking, investment, insurance, permissions
notes: API rate limited to 50 req/10s. RES bulk extract delivers 17 linked files. RES handbook at https://www.fca.org.uk/publication/documents/register-extract-handbook.pdf. Commercial licence required for RES.

title: Met Office HadUK-Grid Gridded Climate Observations
short_description: 1km gridded UK climate observations from 1836 covering temperature, rainfall, sunshine, and frost days.
long_description: HadUK-Grid is the Met Office's official gridded dataset of UK climate observations, interpolated from the surface station network onto a 1km grid. Available at 1km, 5km, 12km, and 25km resolutions with daily, monthly, seasonal, and annual aggregations. Variables include max/min/mean temperature, rainfall, sunshine, snow, and frost days. Some variables from 1836. Accessed via CEDA Archive. Used for climate trend analysis and environmental modelling.
record_count: ~320,000 grid cells x multiple variables x 180+ years
access_method: Free bulk download — free registration required via CEDA Archive (ceda.ac.uk)
schema_fields: longitude|float|Longitude of grid cell centre; latitude|float|Latitude of grid cell centre; time|date|Observation date; tas|float|Mean air temperature degrees C; tasmax|float|Max temperature degrees C; tasmin|float|Min temperature degrees C; rainfall|float|Total rainfall mm; sun|float|Sunshine hours
tags: climate, weather, temperature, rainfall, sunshine, UK, environment, meteorology, grid, historical
notes: NetCDF format requires specialist tooling (Python xarray). CEDA free registration required. Current version HadUK-Grid v1.2.

title: London Fire Brigade Incident Records
short_description: Every incident attended by London Fire Brigade since January 2009 with location, type, and response details.
long_description: Comprehensive record of every incident attended by the London Fire Brigade since 1 January 2009. Covers fire incidents, special services (road traffic collisions, flooding, animal rescues), and false alarms. Each record includes incident date, type, borough, ward, postcode, property type, number of casualties, and pumping appliances deployed. Updated monthly. Over 2 million records to date. Essential for fire risk analysis, urban planning, and emergency service benchmarking.
schema_fields: IncidentNumber|string|Unique incident identifier; DateOfCall|date|Date incident reported; CalYear|integer|Calendar year; HourOfCall|integer|Hour of call 0-23; IncidentGroup|string|High-level category e.g. Fire/Special Service/False Alarm; StopCodeDescription|string|Detailed incident type; Borough|string|London borough; Ward|string|Electoral ward; Postcode|string|Incident postcode; NumPumpsAttending|integer|Number of fire engines dispatched
sample_rows: 000001-2023,01/01/2023,2023,2,Special Service,Lift Release,Tower Hamlets,Bethnal Green,E2 6PQ,1; 000002-2023,01/01/2023,2023,3,False Alarm,Automatic False Alarm,Westminster,St James's,SW1A 2AA,1; 000003-2023,01/01/2023,2023,4,Fire,Primary Fire,Hackney,Hackney Central,E8 1LA,2
tags: fire, incidents, London, emergency response, LFB, safety, borough, ward, rescue
notes: Separate dataset for mobilisation records (individual appliance movements). 523 MB total download. Dashboard available on London Datastore for ward/postcode drill-down.

title: London Fire Brigade Mobilisation Records
short_description: Every fire engine deployed to an LFB incident since 2009 including station, response times, and appliance details.
long_description: Detailed record of every fire engine (pumping appliance) sent to an LFB incident since January 2009. Includes the appliance type, station it was deployed from, turnout time, arrival time, and whether it was the first pump attending. Links to incident records via IncidentNumber. Enables analysis of response time compliance, station performance, and resource deployment patterns. Over 5 million mobilisation records.
schema_fields: IncidentNumber|string|Links to incident records; CalYear|integer|Calendar year; DateAndTimeMobilised|datetime|When appliance was mobilised; DateAndTimeArrived|datetime|When appliance arrived at scene; TurnoutTime|integer|Seconds from mobilisation to leaving station; TravelTime|integer|Seconds from leaving station to arriving; Resource_Code|string|Appliance identifier; DeployedFromStation_Name|string|Station name
tags: fire brigade, response times, mobilisation, stations, LFB, London, emergency services
notes: Links to LFB Incident Records via IncidentNumber. Total download ~433 MB. Useful for response time benchmarking against national standards.

title: London Fire Brigade Home Fire Safety Visits
short_description: Number of free home fire safety visits carried out by LFB across London boroughs each year.
long_description: Annual and quarterly counts of home fire safety visits (HFSVs) carried out by the London Fire Brigade, broken down by London borough. HFSVs are free visits where LFB staff provide fire safety advice and install smoke alarms. Data covers the number of visits, smoke alarms fitted, and whether the household was considered vulnerable. Used to track prevention activity and borough-level targeting of at-risk households.
schema_fields: Borough|string|London borough name; Year|integer|Financial year; Quarter|string|Quarter e.g. Q1 April-June; HFSVs_Total|integer|Total home fire safety visits; SmokeAlarmsInstalled|integer|Number of smoke alarms fitted; VulnerableHouseholds|integer|Visits to vulnerable households
tags: fire safety, home visits, smoke alarms, London, prevention, LFB, borough, vulnerable
notes: Companion dataset to LFB Incident Records. Useful for correlating prevention activity with fire incidence rates.

title: London Fire Brigade Regulatory Fire Safety Inspections
short_description: All regulatory fire safety inspections and enforcement actions by LFB under the Fire Safety Order since 2020.
long_description: Monthly-updated list of fire safety inspections and enforcement visits carried out by LFB Inspecting Officers under the Regulatory Reform (Fire Safety) Order 2005. Covers premises visited, inspection type, enforcement notices issued, and outcome. Updated one month in arrears. Covers the last 5 years of inspection data. Useful for commercial property due diligence, insurance risk assessment, and building safety compliance.
schema_fields: InspectionDate|date|Date of inspection; PremisesName|string|Name of premises inspected; PremisesAddress|string|Address of premises; Borough|string|London borough; InspectionType|string|Type of inspection e.g. Audit/Revisit; EnforcementAction|string|Action taken if any; Outcome|string|Result of inspection
sample_rows: 15/01/2024,City Tower Hotel,1 Tower Bridge Road,Southwark,Audit,None,Satisfactory; 20/01/2024,Canary Wharf Shopping Centre,Canada Square,Tower Hamlets,Enforcement Visit,Enforcement Notice,Non-compliant
tags: fire safety, inspections, enforcement, commercial property, compliance, LFB, London, building safety
notes: Covers Regulatory Reform (Fire Safety) Order 2005 inspections only. Does not include domestic premises. Updated one month in arrears.

title: TfL Public Transport Journeys by Type
short_description: Number of journeys on the London public transport network by TfL reporting period and mode since 2010.
long_description: Total journeys on the TfL public transport network broken down by mode of transport: bus, Underground, DLR, Overground, Tram, Elizabeth line, and cable car. Data is provided by TfL reporting period (13 periods per year). Covers from period 1 2010/11 to present. Does not adjust for varying period lengths. Essential for tracking modal shift, post-COVID recovery, and demand trends across London's transport network.
schema_fields: Period|string|TfL reporting period e.g. 2023/24 P01; Journeys_Bus|integer|Bus journeys millions; Journeys_Underground|integer|Underground journeys millions; Journeys_DLR|integer|DLR journeys millions; Journeys_Overground|integer|Overground journeys millions; Journeys_ElizabethLine|integer|Elizabeth line journeys millions; Journeys_Tram|integer|Tram journeys millions
tags: transport, journeys, bus, underground, tube, TfL, London, modal, DLR, overground
notes: Period lengths vary (P1 and P13 differ). Data not adjusted for period length differences. Useful for year-on-year comparison by period.

title: TfL Santander Cycle Hire Scheme
short_description: Total daily and monthly Santander Cycle Hire hires across London from launch in July 2010 to present.
long_description: Total number of hires of the TfL Santander Cycle Hire Scheme (formerly Barclays Cycle Hire) by day, month, and year from launch on 30 July 2010 to present. Also includes individual journey data available via the TfL API and journey data archive, with start/end station, duration, and bike ID. The scheme has grown from 315 docking stations at launch to over 850. Used for active travel analysis and cycling infrastructure planning.
record_count: ~5,000 rows (daily hires); ~100 million individual journeys in archive
access_method: Free bulk download; journey-level data at cycling.data.tfl.gov.uk
schema_fields: Date|date|Calendar date; NumberOfHires_Day|integer|Total hires that day; NumberOfHires_Month|integer|Total hires that month; NumberOfHires_Year|integer|Total hires that calendar year
tags: cycling, bike hire, Santander, TfL, London, active travel, Barclays, docking stations
notes: Aggregate daily counts in this dataset. Individual journey data (start/end dock, duration, bike ID) available separately at cycling.data.tfl.gov.uk/usage-stats/.

title: TfL Congestion Charge Zone Camera Captures
short_description: Monthly ANPR camera captures and confirmed vehicles seen in the London Congestion Charge Zone from 2010.
long_description: Monthly counts of Automatic Number Plate Recognition (ANPR) camera captures and confirmed unique vehicles seen in the London Congestion Charge Zone during charging hours. Camera captures from July 2010; confirmed vehicle counts from October 2016. Captures represent individual plate reads; confirmed vehicles are unique daily vehicle counts. Useful for traffic flow analysis, compliance monitoring, and urban mobility research.
date_coverage: July 2010 to present (captures); October 2016 to present (confirmed vehicles)
schema_fields: Month|string|Reference month e.g. Jul-2023; CameraCaptures|integer|Total ANPR camera captures during charging hours; ConfirmedVehicles|integer|Unique confirmed vehicles seen in CCZ per day average
tags: congestion charge, ANPR, traffic, London, vehicles, CCZ, TfL, cameras, urban mobility
notes: Does not include ULEZ or LEZ camera data. Captures count individual reads; confirmed vehicles count unique daily entries.

title: TfL Unified API
short_description: Real-time and timetabled data across all TfL modes including arrivals, stops, disruptions, journey planning, and fares.
long_description: TfL's open REST API providing unified access to data across all transport modes: bus, Tube, DLR, Overground, Elizabeth line, Tram, River Bus, National Rail within London, and cycling. Endpoints cover live arrivals, stop information, disruptions, journey planning, fare calculations, and road traffic disruptions. Rate limited to 500 requests per minute with free registration. Used by thousands of third-party apps. Data returned as structured JSON.
access_method: Free API — registration recommended for higher rate limits
schema_fields: id|string|Line or stop identifier; name|string|Stop or line name; vehicleId|string|Vehicle identifier; timeToStation|integer|Seconds until arrival; towards|string|Destination display text; currentLocation|string|Last known location; expectedArrival|datetime|Expected arrival ISO8601; modeName|string|Transport mode
sample_rows: {"id":"490003029W","name":"Aldwych","vehicleId":"LT23","timeToStation":90,"towards":"Liverpool Street","modeName":"bus"}
tags: TfL, transport, API, bus arrivals, tube, London, real-time, journey planner, disruptions
notes: Swagger UI at https://api.tfl.gov.uk/swagger/ui. Register at api-portal.tfl.gov.uk. Some endpoints require paid subscription for higher volumes. Historical timetable bulk data also available separately.

title: UK Power Networks Interruptions Incentive Scheme
short_description: Detailed data on UK Power Networks unplanned power fault events in London, East and South East England.
long_description: UK Power Networks publishes detailed unplanned fault data under the Ofgem Interruptions Incentive Scheme (IIS), covering its distribution network across London, the East of England, and the South East. Each record includes fault start/end time, number of customers affected, restoration stages, cause, and main equipment involved. Useful for energy infrastructure analysis, resilience planning, and supply reliability research.
geographic_coverage: London, East of England, South East England
schema_fields: FaultDate|date|Date fault occurred; FaultStartTime|datetime|Fault start timestamp; FaultEndTime|datetime|Fault restoration timestamp; CustomersAffected|integer|Number of customers interrupted; CauseCode|string|Cause of interruption; EquipmentType|string|Type of equipment involved; LicenceArea|string|UKPN licence area; ExcludedFromIIS|boolean|Whether excluded from scheme metrics
sample_rows: 15/03/2023,15/03/2023 14:23,15/03/2023 16:45,450,Third Party Interference,HV Cable,Eastern,No; 20/03/2023,20/03/2023 09:10,20/03/2023 11:30,1200,Equipment Failure,Transformer,London,No
tags: power, electricity, network, faults, interruptions, UKPN, London, energy, Ofgem, reliability
notes: IIS scheme measures customer interruptions (CI) and customer minutes lost (CML). Data covers all three UKPN licence areas. Companion datasets include LCT connected data and DFES scenarios.

title: UK Power Networks Low Carbon Technologies Connected
short_description: Volume of domestic low carbon technologies (EVs, solar, heat pumps) connected to UK Power Networks grid by LSOA.
long_description: Dataset from UK Power Networks showing volumes of domestic low carbon technology (LCT) connections for both generation and demand devices under 1MW, broken down by Lower Super Output Area (LSOA). Covers EV chargers, solar PV, battery storage, and heat pumps. Used for electric vehicle adoption analysis, grid planning, net zero progress monitoring, and energy transition research.
geographic_coverage: London, East of England, South East England
schema_fields: LSOA_Code|string|ONS LSOA code; LSOA_Name|string|LSOA name; EV_Connections|integer|Number of EV charger connections; Solar_PV_Connections|integer|Number of solar PV installations; Battery_Storage_Connections|integer|Number of battery storage installations; HeatPump_Connections|integer|Number of heat pump installations
tags: EV, electric vehicles, solar PV, heat pumps, low carbon, UKPN, energy transition, LSOA, net zero
notes: Generation and demand connections reported separately. Sub-1MW only. Companion to UKPN DFES scenario forecasts.

title: UK Power Networks Distribution Future Energy Scenarios
short_description: Granular scenario-based forecasts for EVs, heat pumps, and renewable generation across UKPN's network to 2050.
long_description: The Distribution Future Energy Scenarios (DFES) from UK Power Networks provide long-term granular forecasts for key net zero drivers across the UKPN distribution network. Covers EV adoption, heat pump penetration, solar PV generation, battery storage, and district heat networks across multiple scenarios (Leading the Way, Consumer Transformation, Steady Progression). Granular to substation and LSOA level. Published annually.
record_count: ~30,000 LSOAs x 3 scenarios x 30 forecast years
geographic_coverage: London, East of England, South East England
schema_fields: LSOA_Code|string|ONS LSOA geography code; Scenario|string|Scenario name e.g. Leading the Way; Year|integer|Forecast year; EV_Demand_kW|decimal|Forecast EV peak demand kW; HeatPump_Demand_kW|decimal|Forecast heat pump peak demand kW; Solar_Generation_kW|decimal|Forecast solar PV generation kW
sample_rows: E01000001,Leading the Way,2030,850,420,310; E01000001,Steady Progression,2030,520,280,285; E01001234,Consumer Transformation,2035,1200,680,420
tags: energy forecasting, EV, heat pumps, solar, net zero, scenarios, UKPN, distribution, grid, 2050
notes: Aligned with National Grid ESO Future Energy Scenarios. Published annually with updated assumptions. Useful for local authority and DNO planning.

title: Metropolitan Police Service Street-Level Crime Data
short_description: All crimes recorded by the Metropolitan Police Service by type, ward, and month from 2010 to present.
long_description: Monthly crime data for the Metropolitan Police Service area covering all 33 London boroughs, broken down by crime type, borough, and ward. Includes offences such as burglary, vehicle crime, violence, robbery, and drug offences. Data published via data.police.uk and the London Datastore. Also available at the individual street segment level via the police.uk API and bulk CSV downloads. Over 10 million records from 2010.
schema_fields: LookUp_BoroughName|string|London borough name; LookUp_WardName|string|Electoral ward name; MonthYear|string|Reference month e.g. 202301; MajorText|string|Major crime category; MinorText|string|Minor crime sub-category; Count|integer|Number of offences recorded
sample_rows: Hackney,Hackney Central,202301,Violence,Harassment,145; Tower Hamlets,Bethnal Green,202301,Theft,Theft from Motor Vehicle,67; Westminster,St James's,202301,Burglary,Residential Burglary,32
tags: crime, police, Metropolitan Police, London, borough, ward, burglary, violence, theft
notes: Also available at street segment level via data.police.uk/data (CSV) and API. MPS also publishes stop and search data separately.

title: GLA London Plan Annual Monitoring Report Data
short_description: Annual data tables tracking delivery of the London Plan policies across housing, employment, environment, and transport.
long_description: Annual Monitoring Report (AMR) data tables published by the GLA tracking performance against London Plan policies. Covers housing starts and completions, affordable housing delivery, employment floorspace, green infrastructure, air quality, and transport modal share by borough. Each AMR covers one financial year. Data tables in Excel with consistent borough-level breakdowns going back to 2004. Essential for planning research and local authority benchmarking.
schema_fields: Borough|string|London borough name; Indicator|string|London Plan indicator reference; IndicatorDescription|string|Description of the indicator; Value|decimal|Reported value; Unit|string|Unit of measurement; Year|string|Financial year e.g. 2023/24
sample_rows: Hackney,H1,Net additional homes,2456,units,2023/24; Tower Hamlets,H2a,Affordable homes completed,890,units,2023/24; Southwark,H3,Housing starts,3100,units,2023/24
tags: planning, London Plan, housing, employment, AMR, monitoring, GLA, borough, development
notes: AMR 21 covers 2023/24. Published in arrears approximately 12 months after financial year end. Companion to GLA Housing in London Evidence Base.

title: GLA Patients Registered at a GP Practice
short_description: Modelled counts of patients registered at London GP practices by age, sex, and local authority of residence.
long_description: Modelled quarterly counts of patients registered at GP practices across London, broken down by single year of age (0-95+), sex, and local authority of residence. Derived from NHS Digital data using modelling to allocate patients to their residential local authority. Published quarterly. Covers all 33 London boroughs plus City of London. Used for health service planning and population health analysis.
record_count: ~33 LAs x 192 age/sex groups x quarterly
schema_fields: LA_Code|string|ONS local authority code; LA_Name|string|Local authority name; Age|integer|Single year of age 0-95+; Sex|string|Male/Female/Total; RegisteredPatients|integer|Modelled registered patient count; Quarter|string|Reference quarter e.g. 2024Q1

tags: GP, patients, health, demographics, London, NHS, registration, primary care, borough
notes: Modelled data — not directly from GP registration records. Based on NHS Digital practice-level data. 108 MB total download.

title: GLA Housing in London Evidence Base
short_description: Comprehensive annual evidence report with data on London housing supply, affordability, tenure, and homelessness.
long_description: Annual report and data tables from the GLA summarising key housing patterns and trends in London. Covers housing supply and completions, house prices and rents, affordability ratios, tenure mix, social housing lettings, overcrowding, homelessness, and rough sleeping. Data drawn from multiple official sources including HMLR, MHCLG, ONS, and the GLA's own surveys. Each edition includes Excel data tables by borough. Published since 2008.

title: London Brownfield Land Register

title: DfT Road Safety Data (STATS19)
short_description: All personal injury road collisions in Great Britain reported to police since 1979 with vehicle and casualty details.
long_description: STATS19 is the national road collision reporting system used by all UK police forces. The open dataset covers every personal injury road collision in Great Britain reported to police from 1979 to present. Three linked tables: accidents (location, severity, date, conditions), vehicles (type, manoeuvre, driver details), and casualties (age, sex, injury severity). Updated annually. Over 2 million accident records. Critical for road safety research, transport planning, and insurance.

title: ONS Annual Survey of Hours and Earnings (ASHE)
short_description: UK earnings and hours worked by occupation, industry, age, sex, and region — the definitive annual wage survey.
long_description: The Annual Survey of Hours and Earnings (ASHE) is the ONS's most comprehensive source on UK earnings structure. Carried out each April, it provides estimates of gross weekly pay, hourly pay, annual pay, and hours worked for full-time and part-time employees. Breakdowns available by 4-digit SOC occupation, industry (SIC), age group, sex, region, and public/private sector. Available from 1997 to present. Also available via Nomis API for custom extractions.

Endpoints cover current water level, river flow, rainfall, tidal readings, flood warnings and alerts, and 3-day flood risk forecasts. Data updated at 15-minute intervals. title: Bank of England Statistical Database

long_description: The Bank of England's statistical database provides access to hundreds of UK monetary and financial data series including the base rate (Bank Rate), exchange rates, monetary aggregates (M0, M4), consumer credit, mortgage lending, gilt yields, and balance of payments. title: NHS Hospital Episode Statistics (HES)

long_description: Hospital Episode Statistics (HES) is the definitive record-level dataset covering all NHS hospital activity in England: Admitted Patient Care (inpatients), Outpatients, Accident and Emergency, and Critical Care. Each record contains diagnosis codes (ICD-10), procedure codes (OPCS-4), admission method, length of stay, specialty, provider, and patient demographics (anonymised). Covers over 20 million admissions per year. Access via the DARS application process. A powerful resource for health economics, epidemiology, and service planning.

title: ONS Consumer Price Index (CPI and CPIH)

title: NHS Referral to Treatment Waiting Times
short_description: Monthly NHS England waiting times for consultant-led treatment showing 18-week target performance by specialty.
long_description: Monthly NHS England Referral to Treatment (RTT) statistics covering waiting times for incomplete and completed pathways across all acute hospital trusts and specialties. Shows the number of patients waiting 0-18 weeks, 18-52 weeks, and over 52 weeks (breach of target). Includes trust-level and national aggregates by specialty. The NHS target is 92% of patients treated within 18 weeks. Data from August 2012. Essential for NHS performance monitoring and health economics.
record_count: ~250 NHS trusts x 32 specialties x monthly
schema_fields: Period|string|Reference month YYYY-MM; Provider_Org_Code|string|NHS trust code; Provider_Org_Name|string|NHS trust name; Treatment_Function|string|Medical specialty; TotalWaiting|integer|Total patients on incomplete pathway; Waiting_0_to_18_Weeks|integer|Patients waiting under 18 weeks; Waiting_Over_52_Weeks|integer|Patients waiting over 52 weeks; PercentWithin18Weeks|decimal|Percentage within 18-week target

notes: 18-week target: 92% of patients should wait no more than 18 weeks from referral to treatment. Post-COVID backlog has substantially increased waiting lists. Separate publication for mental health waits.

title: Office of Rail and Road Passenger Journeys and Revenue
short_description: Quarterly rail passenger journeys, revenue, and station usage statistics for Great Britain from 1997.
long_description: The Office of Rail and Road (ORR) publishes quarterly and annual statistics on rail passenger journeys, revenue, train kilometres operated, and station entries/exits for Great Britain. Covers all franchised and open-access operators. Also publishes station usage estimates for over 2,500 stations, delay attribution statistics, and safety performance data. Data from 1997. Quarterly journey totals updated within 12 weeks of quarter end.
schema_fields: Period|string|Financial year and quarter; Operator|string|Train operating company; Journeys_Millions|decimal|Passenger journeys millions; Revenue_GBP_Millions|decimal|Passenger revenue GBP millions; TrainKm_Millions|decimal|Train kilometres operated millions; PPM|decimal|Public Performance Measure on-time %
tags: rail, train, passengers, journeys, ORR, Great Britain, revenue, stations, transport, operator
notes: Station usage data published annually showing entries and exits per station. ORR data portal at dataportal.orr.gov.uk provides interactive query tool.

title: Civil Aviation Authority UK Airport Statistics
short_description: Monthly and annual passenger, cargo, and movement statistics for all UK airports from the CAA since 1990.
long_description: The Civil Aviation Authority publishes monthly airport statistics covering passenger numbers (terminal and transit), freight, mail, and aircraft movements for all reporting UK airports. Also includes route-level statistics showing passenger numbers for each city-pair. Annual statistics available from 1990. The CAA collects data directly from airport operators. Covers over 60 UK airports. Key source for aviation sector analysis, route planning, and tourism research.
schema_fields: Airport|string|Airport name; Year|integer|Reference year; Month|string|Reference month; Total_Pax|integer|Total terminal passengers; International_Pax|integer|International terminal passengers; Domestic_Pax|integer|Domestic terminal passengers; Total_Freight_Tonnes|decimal|Total freight and mail tonnes; Total_Movements|integer|Total air transport movements
tags: aviation, airports, passengers, Heathrow, Gatwick, cargo, CAA, flights, UK, movements
notes: Route-level statistics show passenger numbers by city-pair. International route data published separately. Heathrow and Gatwick publish their own detailed statistics as well.

title: Environment Agency UK Air Quality — Automatic Urban and Rural Network (AURN)
short_description: Hourly and daily air pollution readings from 170+ AURN monitoring stations across the UK since 1972.
long_description: The Automatic Urban and Rural Network (AURN) is the UK's primary air quality monitoring network, operated by the Environment Agency and Defra. Provides hourly readings for major air pollutants including NO2, NOx, PM10, PM2.5, O3, SO2, CO, and benzene from over 170 stations across the UK. Data ratified annually. Historical data available from 1972 for some stations. Also accessible via UK-AIR API and downloadable as annual CSV files by site. Essential for air quality compliance, health research, and environmental analysis.
provider: Department for Environment Food and Rural Affairs (Defra) / Environment Agency
record_count: 170+ stations x hourly readings; ~50 years of data
access_method: Free bulk download; Free API at uk-air.defra.gov.uk/data/openair
schema_fields: site_id|string|AURN site code; site_name|string|Station name; date|datetime|Reading timestamp; NO2|decimal|Nitrogen dioxide micrograms per m3; NOx|decimal|Nitrogen oxides micrograms per m3; PM10|decimal|Particulate matter 10 microns micrograms per m3; PM25|decimal|Particulate matter 2.5 microns micrograms per m3; O3|decimal|Ozone micrograms per m3
tags: air quality, pollution, NO2, PM2.5, PM10, ozone, AURN, Defra, environment, health, monitoring
notes: openair R package provides easy access to AURN data. Annual ratification process updates provisional readings. Local authority supplementary monitoring networks available separately via local councils.

title: Historic England Listed Buildings Register
short_description: All Listed Buildings in England by grade, description, and OS coordinates — over 375,000 entries.
long_description: Historic England's National Heritage List for England (NHLE) provides details of all designated heritage assets including Listed Buildings, Scheduled Monuments, Registered Parks and Gardens, Registered Battlefields, and Protected Wreck Sites. Listed Buildings are graded I (most significant), II*, and II. Each entry includes the property name, address, listing date, description, OS grid reference, and polygon boundary. Over 375,000 Listed Buildings. Available as a free download.

title: Planning Inspectorate Appeal Decisions
short_description: All planning appeal decisions determined by the Planning Inspectorate in England from 1989 including case type and outcome.
long_description: The Planning Inspectorate publishes data on all planning and enforcement appeal decisions in England. Includes appeal reference, local planning authority, case type (householder, major development, enforcement), inspector's decision (allowed/dismissed), appeal method (hearing, inquiry, written representations), and decision date. Over 1 million records from 1989. Useful for planning policy analysis, appeal success rate benchmarking, and development viability research.

notes: Search and download tool at acp.planninginspectorate.gov.uk. Scraping may be needed for detailed decision letters. FORMAT: PDF for decision letters — requires AI extraction.

title: Insolvency Service Individual and Company Insolvency Statistics
short_description: Quarterly statistics on company liquidations, administrations, and individual insolvencies in England and Wales since 1960.
long_description: The Insolvency Service publishes quarterly statistics on business and personal insolvencies in England and Wales. Business insolvencies include compulsory liquidations, creditors' voluntary liquidations, administrations, and company voluntary arrangements (CVAs). Personal insolvencies include bankruptcy orders, debt relief orders, and individual voluntary arrangements (IVAs). Regional and industry breakdowns available. Time series from 1960 for company insolvencies. A key economic indicator of financial distress.
record_count: ~250,000 records per year across all insolvency types
schema_fields: Quarter|string|Reference quarter e.g. 2024Q1; InsolvencyType|string|Type e.g. Compulsory Liquidation/CVL/Administration/IVA/DRO; Count|integer|Number of insolvencies; Region|string|Government office region; IndustrySIC|string|SIC division for company insolvencies

notes: Individual insolvency statistics also available monthly via London Gazette (statutory notices). Companion dataset to Companies House dissolved companies data.

title: London Gazette Statutory Insolvency and Company Notices
short_description: All statutory company, insolvency, and personal notices published in the London Gazette — published as structured XML since 1665.
long_description: The London Gazette is the UK's official newspaper of record, publishing statutory notices including company winding-up petitions, liquidation notices, administration appointments, bankruptcy orders, change of name notices, and other legally required announcements. Available as structured XML dating back to the 1800s, with a REST API providing access to recent notices. Over 3 million notices in the online archive. Each notice includes the subject entity, notice type, date, reference, and full text.
record_count: ~3 million notices in archive; ~1,000 new notices per day
access_method: Free API — registration recommended; Free bulk download of historical data

Includes first-time buyer relief claims. Available quarterly from 2005. Useful for housing market analysis, tax policy research, and understanding property transaction volumes at regional level.

title: ONS Labour Force Survey Microdata
short_description: UK's largest household survey covering employment, unemployment, hours, earnings, and economic activity — quarterly microdata.
long_description: The Labour Force Survey (LFS) is the ONS's largest household survey, conducted quarterly and covering approximately 90,000 individuals per quarter. Provides estimates of employment, unemployment, economic inactivity, hours worked, earnings, qualifications, occupation, industry, and work conditions for the UK. Microdata available via UK Data Service (UKDS) for researchers. Aggregate statistics published freely. The LFS is the source for the UK's official unemployment rate.

title: DfT Vehicle Licensing Statistics
short_description: Quarterly licensed vehicle counts in Great Britain by type, fuel, body style, keeper type, and local authority.
long_description: Quarterly statistics from the Driver and Vehicle Licensing Agency (DVLA) on the number of licensed vehicles in Great Britain, broken down by vehicle type, fuel type (petrol/diesel/electric/hybrid), body style, age band, and geographic area including local authority. Covers all vehicles on the DVLA database including cars, motorcycles, light goods vehicles, heavy goods vehicles, and buses. Essential for EV adoption monitoring, transport emissions analysis, and fleet planning.

title: DEFRA UK Greenhouse Gas Emissions Inventory
short_description: Annual UK greenhouse gas emissions by sector, source, and gas from 1990 — the official national inventory submission to the UNFCCC.
long_description: The UK's official annual greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions inventory, submitted to the UN Framework Convention on Climate Change (UNFCCC). Covers CO2, CH4, N2O, HFCs, PFCs, SF6, and NF3 emissions by sector (energy, transport, buildings, industry, agriculture, waste, and LULUCF). Data from 1990 to present in a consistent time series enabling trend analysis. Published by Defra/BEIS annually. UK carbon budget progress tracked against this inventory.
provider: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero / Defra
schema_fields: Year|integer|Reference year; Sector|string|High-level sector e.g. Energy Supply/Transport; Sub_Sector|string|Detailed sub-sector; Source|string|Emission source description; Gas|string|GHG type e.g. CO2/CH4/N2O; EmissionsMtCO2e|decimal|Emissions million tonnes CO2 equivalent

title: BEIS UK Energy Trends
short_description: Quarterly UK energy production, supply, and consumption statistics by fuel type including renewables from 1970.
long_description: UK Energy Trends is the quarterly publication from the Department for Energy Security and Net Zero covering energy production, transformation, and consumption across all fuels: oil, natural gas, coal, nuclear, wind, solar, bioenergy, and other renewables. Includes electricity generation mix, energy prices, and trade statistics. Data from 1970 for most series. Monthly provisional data also available. The primary reference source for UK energy statistics.
provider: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero
schema_fields: Period|string|Reference quarter or year; FuelType|string|Energy source e.g. Wind/Gas/Nuclear/Solar; Flow|string|Production/Consumption/Imports/Exports; Value|decimal|Energy in TWh or PJ; Unit|string|Unit of measurement

notes: Companion Digest of UK Energy Statistics (DUKES) provides full annual dataset. Sub-national electricity and gas consumption by local authority published separately by BEIS.

title: National Grid ESO Historic Demand and Generation Data
short_description: Half-hourly electricity demand and generation mix for Great Britain from 2009 published by the Electricity System Operator.
long_description: National Grid ESO publishes half-hourly electricity demand and generation data for Great Britain, covering total system demand, transmission system demand, and generation by fuel type (gas, nuclear, wind, solar, interconnectors, hydro, coal, biomass). Also includes carbon intensity data. Available via API and bulk CSV download. Data from 2009 for demand; 2008 for generation mix. Real-time data also available. Foundational for energy market analysis, trading, and carbon accounting.
record_count: ~180,000 half-hourly records per year x multiple fuel types

title: Ofcom Communications Market Data
short_description: Annual UK communications market statistics covering broadband, mobile, TV, radio, and postal services.
long_description: Ofcom's annual Communications Market Report provides comprehensive statistics on the UK communications sector: fixed broadband take-up and speeds, mobile subscribers and coverage, TV viewing and subscription, radio listening, and postal volumes. Includes market share by operator, revenue, and geographic coverage data. Also includes Connected Nations reports with local authority-level broadband coverage and speeds. Published annually in August.

title: ICO Data Protection Fee Register and Enforcement Actions
short_description: Register of all organisations paying the ICO data protection fee plus enforcement notices and fines from 2018.
long_description: The Information Commissioner's Office (ICO) publishes the register of all organisations that have paid the mandatory data protection fee under the UK GDPR/Data Protection Act 2018. Includes organisation name, reference number, tier, payment date, and registered address. Also publishes enforcement actions including fines, warnings, and enforcement notices with case details. Over 900,000 organisations on the register. Published as downloadable CSV.

title: HMRC UK Trade in Goods Statistics
short_description: Monthly UK imports and exports of goods by commodity code, partner country, and UK port of exit/entry.
long_description: HMRC's UK Trade Info data covers monthly imports and exports of all goods, broken down by 8-digit commodity code (CN8), partner country, and method of transport (sea, air, road, rail). Data from 1997 for EU trade; 2000 for non-EU. Covers over 9,000 commodity codes and 230 countries. Available via the HMRC Trade Info portal with bulk CSV downloads. The primary source for UK international trade analysis post-Brexit.

title: GLA London Atmospheric Emissions Inventory (LAEI)

Sources include road transport, aircraft, shipping, rail, construction, domestic heating, and industry. Pollutants covered: NOx, NO2, PM10, PM2.5, CO2, and volatile organic compounds. Published approximately every 5 years with the 2022 edition being the latest. Used by the GLA, TfL, and London boroughs for air quality management, ULEZ analysis, and planning.

title: GLA London Election Results

title: ONS Crime Survey for England and Wales (CSEW)
short_description: Annual household survey measuring crime victimisation in England and Wales independent of police recording — from 1981.
long_description: The Crime Survey for England and Wales (CSEW), formerly the British Crime Survey, is a continuous household survey measuring the experience of crime among adults aged 16+ (and 10-15 in a separate module). Covers personal, household, and fraud crime. Because it measures crime directly experienced by respondents rather than crimes reported to police, it captures offences not reported to or recorded by the police. Sample of approximately 35,000 households per year. Microdata available via UK Data Service.

title: HMLR Overseas Companies Ownership Register
short_description: Register of all UK property owned by overseas companies including beneficial ownership details where disclosed.
long_description: Following the Economic Crime (Transparency and Enforcement) Act 2022, overseas companies owning UK property must register with Companies House and disclose their beneficial owners. HMLR also publishes data on all properties in England and Wales where the registered proprietor is an overseas company. Approximately 100,000 properties. The dataset includes title number, overseas company name, country of incorporation, date of registration, and property address. Key for anti-money-laundering analysis.

title: ONS House Price Statistics for Small Areas (HPSSA)
short_description: Annual median house prices for all wards, local authorities, and parliamentary constituencies in England and Wales from 2002.
long_description: Annual house price statistics for small areas in England and Wales published by the ONS, providing median and lower quartile house prices at ward, local authority, county, and parliamentary constituency level. Based on HMLR Price Paid Data. Covers all residential property types and individual property types (detached, semi-detached, terraced, flat). Annual data from 2002. Enables granular housing affordability analysis not available in the UK HPI.

title: Valuation Office Agency (VOA) Private Rental Market Statistics
short_description: Annual and quarterly private sector rental prices for all bedrooms sizes by local authority in England from 2011.
long_description: The Valuation Office Agency (VOA) Private Rental Market Statistics provide annual and quarterly data on private sector rental prices across England, broken down by number of bedrooms, local authority, and broad rental market area. Data collected from letting agents and landlords for Local Housing Allowance purposes. Provides median, lower quartile, and upper quartile rents. Annual data from 2011. Key source for rental market analysis, housing benefit policy, and affordability research.
record_count: ~350 local authorities x 5 bedroom sizes x annual

tags: rents, private rental, housing, affordability, local authority, VOA, landlord, tenants, England
notes: Data collected for Local Housing Allowance rate-setting. Broad Rental Market Areas (BRMAs) also published. Scotland, Wales, and NI publish separate rental statistics.

long_description: Bulk snapshot and real-time streaming data of all company officer appointments and resignations at UK companies registered at Companies House. Includes directors, secretaries, LLP members, and other officer types. Contains officer name, date of birth, nationality, country of residence, appointment date, resignation date, and occupation. Separately covers trade between UK and EU/non-EU countries. Financial services exports are particularly significant for London — the UK is the world's largest exporter of financial services. Data from 1999 for most categories. Published quarterly approximately 12 weeks after reference period.

title: Met Office Synoptic Observation Weather Data (MIDAS)
short_description: Hourly and daily weather observations from 5,000+ UK Met Office surface stations from 1853 via the CEDA Archive.
long_description: The Met Office Integrated Data Archive System (MIDAS) provides access to weather observations from over 5,000 UK surface weather stations dating back to 1853. Includes hourly and daily measurements of temperature, wind speed and direction, pressure, humidity, visibility, cloud cover, rainfall, sunshine, and snow depth. Available via the CEDA Archive (free registration required). MIDAS Open is a subset available without registration. Foundational for UK climate research, engineering design, and operational risk analysis.

Available as part of the Price Paid Data (Category B transactions) and separately via the UK Land Registry Open Data platform. Includes transaction price, date, address, property type, and whether freehold or leasehold. Fewer records than residential data but high-value. Essential for commercial real estate market analysis.

title: ONS UK GDP Quarterly National Accounts
short_description: Official quarterly UK GDP estimates by expenditure, income, and output approach from 1955 with regional GVA.
long_description: ONS quarterly national accounts providing estimates of UK Gross Domestic Product (GDP) by three approaches: expenditure (consumption, investment, trade), income (wages, profits, taxes), and output (by industry). Also includes regional Gross Value Added (GVA) at local authority level published annually. Quarterly GDP estimates from 1955. The ONS publishes a preliminary estimate within 28 days of quarter end, revised estimates at 56 and 84 days, and final estimates in the annual Blue Book.
geographic_coverage: United Kingdom (regional GVA for England and Wales)

title: DLUHC Local Authority Revenue and Capital Outturn
short_description: Annual local authority spending and revenue statistics for all English councils covering income, expenditure, and reserves.
long_description: DLUHC publishes annual Revenue Outturn (RO) and Capital Outturn (CO) statistics for all English local authorities covering total spending, service-level expenditure, grant income, council tax income, borrowing, and usable reserves. Covers 319 billing authorities and over 400 precepting authorities. Data collected via the Revenue Account Budget/Outturn return. Essential for council financial analysis, fiscal stress modelling, and public finance research.

title: London Ambulance Service Incident Data
short_description: Annual London Ambulance Service call and response data by incident category and borough including response times.
long_description: The London Ambulance Service (LAS) publishes data on emergency calls responded to across Greater London, including incident categories (Category 1: life-threatening to Category 4: non-urgent), response times against national standards, and borough-level demand. Also publishes mental health-related call data. Category 1 target is 7 minutes mean response; Category 2 is 18 minutes mean. Published on the London Datastore. Useful for healthcare planning and emergency services research.

title: ONS Small Area Income Estimates
short_description: Annual net household income and income inequality estimates for all Middle Super Output Areas in England and Wales.
long_description: ONS model-based estimates of total annual net household income (before and after housing costs) and median and mean income for all Middle Super Output Areas (MSOAs) in England and Wales, approximately 7,200 geographies averaging 8,000 residents each. Also includes Gini coefficient and income quintile share ratios as measures of inequality. Data from financial year 2002/03. Published annually. Used for deprivation analysis, retail catchment modelling, and planning.

title: MHCLG English Indices of Multiple Deprivation
short_description: Official deprivation rankings for all 32,844 Lower Super Output Areas in England across 7 deprivation domains.
long_description: The English Indices of Multiple Deprivation (IMD) are the official measure of relative deprivation for Lower Super Output Areas (LSOAs) in England. The IMD combines 39 indicators across 7 domains: Income, Employment, Education, Health, Crime, Barriers to Housing and Services, and Living Environment. Each LSOA is ranked from 1 (most deprived) to 32,844 (least deprived). Published approximately every 4-5 years; latest edition is IMD 2019. Widely used in public health, planning, and policy research.

Provides monthly estimates of the number of employees and median monthly earnings by local authority, Standard Industrial Classification section, and age group. Available from April 2014. title: GLA London Development Database

Submitted by all 33 London boroughs. Contains planning application details, development type, site address, proposed uses, number of units, tenure mix (market/affordable/social rent), developer, and development status. Covers permissions from 2004 and completions from 2004. Essential for housing supply monitoring and planning research.

title: UK Biobank Research Resource

Includes self-reported questionnaire data, physical measurements, cognitive tests, imaging data, genetic array data, and linked electronic health records (NHS hospital, cancer registry, death records, primary care). The world's most powerful health resource for research into common diseases. Access requires application and approval.

title: Companies House Register of Overseas Entities

All overseas legal entities that own UK land or property must register and disclose their beneficial owners. The register contains overseas entity name, country of incorporation, registered office, nature of control, and beneficial owner details. title: TfL Road Network TLRN Performance Data

title: ONS Foreign Direct Investment Statistics

title: Zoopla UK Property Market Listings Data

title: London pubs audit
short_description: As part of the Cultural Infrastructure Plan, an annual audit of pubs using the Inter-Departmental Business Register data is produced. The overall number of...
long_description: As part of the Cultural Infrastructure Plan, an annual audit of pubs using the Inter-Departmental Business Register data is produced. The overall number of pubs in London has remained largely unchanged since 2018. There were 3,535 pubs in London in 2023. Overall employee numbers increased in 2023 for the first time since the pandemic in 2020. In 2023, there were 49,000 jobs in London pubs, above the previous peak in 2020 at 48,000 jobs. The share of jobs in large pubs (with more than 10 employees) reached 85% in 2023, while the share of jobs in smaller pubs dropped to 14%, its lowest value in the data going back to 2001. long_description: The Mayor of London committed to developing a London Wellbeing and Sustainability Measure in his 2021 manifesto . This page hosts a summary report that accompanied publication of the first iteration, a user guide created by the Centre for Thriving Places, as well as the data for the 64 constituent indicators that make up the London Wellbeing and Sustainability Measure in one spreadsheet. title: Tourism estimates for London in 2019 and forecasts for 2020
short_description: London’s Tourism Direct GVA (TDGVA) in 2019 is estimated by taking London’s share of the latest ONS regional TDGVA publication (2013) and multiplying it...
long_description: London’s Tourism Direct GVA (TDGVA) in 2019 is estimated by taking London’s share of the latest ONS regional TDGVA publication (2013) and multiplying it with the latest TDGVA estimate (2017) for the UK. The resulting figure is then multiplied with a ratio of London spend data from VisitBritain’s International Passenger Surveys from 2019 and 2017. This approach assumes 1) that London’s share of UK TDGVA has remained constant from 2013 to 2017 and 2) a constant relation between spend and GVA from 2017 to 2019. The annual VisitBritain forecast for the volume and value of inbound tourism to the UK is issued in December each year. title: Data Centre Profiles
short_description: This dataset shows the half-hourly load profiles of identified data centres within UK Power Networks' licence areas. The loads have been determined using...
long_description: This dataset shows the half-hourly load profiles of identified data centres within UK Power Networks' licence areas. The loads have been determined using actual demand data from connected sites within UK Power Networks' licence areas, from 1 January 2023 onwards. Loads are expressed proportionally, by comparing the half-hourly observed import power seen across the site's meter point(s), against the meter's maximum import capacity. Units for both measures are apparent power, in kilovolt amperes (kVA). To protect the identity of the sites, data points have been anonymised and only the site's voltage level information - and our estimation of the data centre type - has been provided.

long_description: A Business Improvement District (BID) is a defined area within which local businesses are required to pay an additional levy. There are now more people in poverty in London who are in a working family than in a workless family. The reverse was true 30 years ago. Insecure forms of work such as part-time work and self-employment are linked to poverty. Ethnic minority workers and those with no educational qualifications are most likely to be working and in poverty. One-in-10 employed Londoners report a health condition, leading to around 1 million of work hours lost through sickness absences annually. title: Secondary Substation Feeding Areas
short_description: A shapefile containing the postcode areas supplied by secondary substations. The dataset is generalised using our GIS customer connectivity model. Each...
long_description: A shapefile containing the postcode areas supplied by secondary substations. The dataset is generalised using our GIS customer connectivity model. Each Secondary Substation's connected LV Network is traced out to its electrical extents, including all connected customers. Using the geographic position of customer connections and key network points of both the Substation being traced and surrounding Substation's network, a Voronoi diagram is created. Each Voronoi polygon that intersects with the traced network is joined together to produce a single polygon, depicting the Supply Zone. This dataset is redacted in that any secondary sites with less than five customers will not be included to protect privacy.

long_description: In January 2022 the Mayor published his Skills Roadmap for London. The Roadmap sets the direction of travel for adult education in London over the Mayoral term and beyond, including future plans for the approximately £320 million per year Adult Education Budget (AEB). The Roadmap was developed based on extensive consultation with stakeholders, communities and businesses. The GLA engaged over 100 organisations through a written consultation and stakeholder roundtables, including local authorities, learning providers and employers and businesses. The GLA also captured the views of almost 1000 Londoners from key target groups through community conversations hosted by community organisations across London. title: Green Skills Adult Education Provision in London
short_description: The GLA commissioned independent research into the green skills which are expected to grow in demand as London moves to net zero. It uses GLA Economics’...
long_description: The GLA commissioned independent research into the green skills which are expected to grow in demand as London moves to net zero. It uses GLA Economics’ Identifying Green Occupations to identify green skills currently being funded through the Adult Education Budget (AEB), along with relevant Level 4 and 5 qualifications and apprenticeship data. The report includes interviews with employers and providers which capture some of key challenges and opportunities to growing demand and supply for green skills provision. It sets out a number of recommendations to support the further growth and development of green skills provision in the capital.

short_description: Quantitative research with London's SMEs undertaken by the GLA's Opinion Research team. Online survey carried out by YouGov for the GLA between 5th–19th...
long_description: Quantitative research with London's SMEs undertaken by the GLA's Opinion Research team. Online survey carried out by YouGov for the GLA between 5th–19th March 2021, with a response of 1,012 London businesses (owners or senior decision makers only). Sample weighted to be representative of all London businesses by size and sector , based on October 2020 Business population estimates data. Main objectives: To understand the immediate impact of Brexit and the trade deal on London’s SMEs, including which areas of business are the most/least affected, and which areas of the trade deal are causing the most confusion To assess the appetite of SMEs to continue/increase trading with the EU in light of the trade deal

short_description: The Mayor is committed to measuring social integration in London. Providing evidence-based analysis of the state of social integration will allow him to...
long_description: The Mayor is committed to measuring social integration in London. Providing evidence-based analysis of the state of social integration will allow him to shine a light on the whole city. Accompanying the Social Integration Strategy published in March 2018, the Mayor published a set of measures tracking social integration in London. These measures were selected from existing high-quality datasets, such as large-scale central government surveys, research council surveys, and administrative data. City Hall picked these 18 measures to cover the three main parts of social integration (relationships, participation and equality), choosing measures which are robust over time. short_description: The purpose of this report is to collate literature on the health, social, community, economic and environmental impacts of epidemics, pandemics and other...

title: Lost worker vs. tourism expenditure in the Central Activities Zone (CAZ) during the COVID-19 Pandemic
short_description: During the pandemic, the loss of expenditure in London’s Central Activities Zone (CAZ) due to both workers staying at home and tourists being unable or...
long_description: During the pandemic, the loss of expenditure in London’s Central Activities Zone (CAZ) due to both workers staying at home and tourists being unable or choosing not to visit has had a major impact on the retail, leisure and hospitality sectors. This analysis seeks to investigate the relative importance of these two cohorts to the CAZ. We estimate local expenditure from commuters to the CAZ in 2020, in a scenario with and without Covid-19. We compare this difference to the anticipated drop in inbound and domestic tourist expenditure in 2020, which we estimate by adjusting forecasts for London produced by Visit Britain. long_description: More than 26,500 London households have had their total benefit payment capped since the benefit cap was introduced in April 2013. Nearly half (45 per cent) of all households in Great Britain that have received reduced amounts because of the cap on the total payable, up to February 2015, were in London. Nearly 90 per cent of households with one or two children whose payments have been capped lived in London, whereas only a few households of this size in other regions could have received payments over the cap limit.

title: A Fairer London: The Living Wage in London
short_description: Since 2006 the GLA Group has been implementing the London Living Wage (LLW).The Group continues to include the LLW as a requirement for contracts that are...
long_description: Since 2006 the GLA Group has been implementing the London Living Wage (LLW).The Group continues to include the LLW as a requirement for contracts that are let or renewed. We calculate the Living Wage by a combination of two approaches. The first, developed by the Family Budget Unit (FBU), estimates the costs of a ‘Low Cost but Acceptable’ (LCA) budget for a selection of households and calculates the wage required to meet these costs. This is termed the “Basic Living Costs” approach. The second – the “Income Distribution” approach – simply identifies the median income for London (appropriately weighted for 11 household types) and then takes 60 per cent of it. short_description: Coronavirus affects some members of the population more than others. Emerging evidence suggests that older people, men, people with health conditions such...
long_description: Coronavirus affects some members of the population more than others. Emerging evidence suggests that older people, men, people with health conditions such as respiratory and pulmonary conditions, and people of a Black, Asian Minority Ethnic (BAME) background are at particular risk. There are also a number of other wider public health risk factors that have been found to increase the likelihood of an individual contracting coronavirus. This briefing presents descriptive evidence on a range of these factors, seeking to understand at a London-wide level the proportion of the population affected by each.

Research has shown that the mortality risk from COVID-19 is higher for people of older age groups, for men, for people with pre-existing health conditions, and for people from BAME backgrounds. short_description: Introduction to fertility estimates Fertility refers to the number of live births within an individual or group, influenced by a combination of biological,...
long_description: Introduction to fertility estimates Fertility refers to the number of live births within an individual or group, influenced by a combination of biological, social, cultural, and economic factors. There are several ways to describe fertility rates, but two of the most commonly used are Age-Specific Fertility Rates (ASFR) and Total Fertility Rates (TFR). Age-specific fertility rates (ASFR) measure the number of births per woman within specific age groups. For example, in England, the peak childbearing age is currently 32, with an ASFR of 0.107, meaning 107 babies were born for each 1,000 women aged 32. title: Births by Mother's Country of Birth in London
short_description: The Office for National Statistics (ONS) publishes data on the number of live births by the mother's country of birth in England and Wales each year. Every...
long_description: The Office for National Statistics (ONS) publishes data on the number of live births by the mother's country of birth in England and Wales each year. Every time a birth is registered in England and Wales both parents are required to state their places of birth on their child's birth certificate, and this information is then collated to produce these statistics. In order to make it easier to look at what these data tell us about births in London, and how these have been changing over time, the GLA Demography team has extracted the data which relate to London from the main ONS dataset since 2001 and presented it here in an easily accessible format. For more information about how the ONS produces these statistics, please visit their website: https://www.ons.gov.uk/peoplepopulationandcommunity

short_description: A number of characteristics of individuals are protected under the 2010 Equality Act, in order to limit the discrimination and disadvantage of groups with...
long_description: A number of characteristics of individuals are protected under the 2010 Equality Act, in order to limit the discrimination and disadvantage of groups with one or several shared characteristics. This table brings together a range of sources to present estimates of London's population by gender, age, ethnicity, religion, disability status, country of birth and sexual identity. It also shows population breakdowns for subgroups in each of these categories by broad age group and ethnicity. The socio-economic position of individuals is not a protected characteristic, but is nonetheless an important factor affecting outcomes. The table therefore also includes social class. The latest update showcases London's population as in 2023.

title: London's Population
short_description: Introduction The 2023 mid-year estimate (MYE) is the current official estimate of the population for local authorities in England and Wales. Estimates are...
long_description: Introduction The 2023 mid-year estimate (MYE) is the current official estimate of the population for local authorities in England and Wales. Estimates are produced annually by the Office for National Statistics (ONS) and the 2023 MYE was published on 15 July 2024. Comparison to previous MYE data The previous MYE series (for the period 2012-2020) starts with the 2011 census estimate. Each subsequent year’s population is calculated by adding estimates of births, deaths and migration to the previous year’s population. The 2021 MYE represents a break in this series as it uses the 2021 census as its base. This page gives access to the OLD 2011 London Output Area Classification (LOAC), an open source geospatial classification, using a combination of over 60 variables from the 2011 Census to classify every small area in London within a hierarchical structure. The 2011 London classification used the same variables and methodology as the UK 2011 Area Classifications , but provided a finer level of classification for London. title: Briefing - Universal Credit and Disabled Londoners
short_description: Disabled Londoners have been disproportionately impacted by changes to the tax and benefits system and the introduction of Universal Credit (UC) has hit...
long_description: Disabled Londoners have been disproportionately impacted by changes to the tax and benefits system and the introduction of Universal Credit (UC) has hit disabled Londoners particularly hard. Cuts to civil society organisations and Local Authority budgets have also reduced support for disabled Londoners. This research provides an insight into the ecosystem of organisations in London offering support to disability benefit claimants migrating to UC. It adds further evidence to the design and implementation flaws that makes the process of claiming UC for disabled Londoners so difficult. It includes rich evidence, featuring the voices of people supporting disabled Londoners in illustrating the problems with this system.

long_description: Deaths by local authority of usual residence, numbers and standardised mortality ratios (SMRs) by sex. SMR measures whether the population of an area has a higher or lower number of deaths than expected based on the age profile of the population (more deaths are expected in older populations). The SMR is defined as follows: SMR = (Observed no. of deaths per year)/(Expected no. of deaths per year). SMRs are calculated using the previous year's mid-year population estimates. Live birth figures are used for calculations involving deaths under 1 year. long_description: This analysis assesses the impact of national changes to the tax and welfare system since 2010 on households in London, using analysis commissioned by the GLA City Intelligence Unit and delivered by Landman Economics. The analysis uses micro-simulation modelling techniques to assess the impact on household incomes in London of changes made to welfare, personal taxes and the introduction of the National Living Wage between May 2010 and October 2018. title: Mortality Risk from High Temperatures in London (Triple Jeopardy Mapping)
short_description: A heatwave refers to a prolonged period of unusually hot weather. While there is no standard definition of a heatwave in England, the Met Office generally...
long_description: A heatwave refers to a prolonged period of unusually hot weather. While there is no standard definition of a heatwave in England, the Met Office generally uses the World Meteorological Organization definition of a heatwave, which is "when the daily maximum temperature of more than five consecutive days exceeds the average maximum temperature by 5°C, the normal period being 1961-1990". They are common in the northern and southern hemisphere during summer, and have historically been associated with health problems and an increase in mortality. The urban heat island (UHI) is the phenomenon where temperatures are relatively higher in cities compared to surrounding rural areas due to, for example, the urban surfaces and anthropogenic heat sources.
