title: The Wealth Gap In London
short_description: This GLA Intelligence Update takes a brief look at evidence around the wealth gap in London and examines how this has changed in recent years. Key Findings...
long_description: This GLA Intelligence Update takes a brief look at evidence around the wealth gap in London and examines how this has changed in recent years. Key Findings There is a significant gap between the rich and poor in London, both in terms of their wealth and their income. A higher proportion of the wealthiest households are in the South East of England than in London. Pension wealth accounts for more than half the wealth of the richest ten per cent of the population. In London, the tenth of the population with the highest income have weekly income after housing costs of over £1,000 while people in the lowest tenth have under £94 per week. The gap between rich and poor is growing, with the difference between the average income for the second highest tenth and second lowest tenth growing around 1

long_description: Key findings: More than one in four of London’s children were in poverty in 2010. Child poverty rates have fallen in London on this measure, but are still higher than in any other region of the UK. Four boroughs, Tower Hamlets, Islington, Westminster and Redbridge, include pockets where more than three in five children were in poverty. Over the last four years, most boroughs have seen decreases in the percentage of children in poverty on this measure, though Bromley has seen virtually no change, while Havering and Bexley have seen increases in most years.

title: The Rising Cost of Living and Access to Adult Education in London
short_description: In September 2023, Learning and Work was commissioned by the GLA to conduct research into the impact of the rising cost of living on London’s adult...
long_description: In September 2023, Learning and Work was commissioned by the GLA to conduct research into the impact of the rising cost of living on London’s adult education landscape. GLA data shows that in 2023, 17% of Londoners were ‘struggling financially’, and another 30% were ‘just about managing’. Increased inflation also impacted the cost of doing business for London’s skills providers and Further Education (FE) workforce. This report and the summary report are based upon a survey, depth interviews and focus groups with learners, providers, and third sector organisations that provide and campaign for people on low incomes. The fieldwork took place between November 2023 and March 2024. The research provides an account of the impact of the rising cost of living on London’s FE sector.

title: Model Based Unemployment Estimates
short_description: The Office for National Statistics (ONS) has jointly developed with Professor Ray Chambers of the University of Southampton, a new modelling methodology to...
long_description: The Office for National Statistics (ONS) has jointly developed with Professor Ray Chambers of the University of Southampton, a new modelling methodology to produce modelled estimates of unemployment levels and rates on the International Labour Organisation (ILO) definition for local authority districts and unitary authorities (LAD/UAs). Key findings London has among the lowest proportions of all-working households of any region in the UK, but also below average proportion of workless households. The proportion of households in London with both working and non-working adults has been rising faster than for most of the UK, while workless households have been decreasing, particularly in Inner London. The number of children in workless households has been falling quite sharply, but London is the only region where the proportion of children in workless households is higher than the overall proportion of households that are workless.

long_description: Pension Credit was introduced in October 2003. It is a non-contributory, income-related benefit. Its purposes are: To lift the poorest pensioners out of poverty by providing a contribution to a minimum guaranteed income for those aged 60 and over living in Great Britain. To reward those aged 65 and over who have made modest provision for their retirement. It is not necessary to have paid National Insurance contributions to be eligible. Pension Credit replaced the Minimum Income Guarantee (MIG), which, itself, replaced Income Support for older people in April 1999. title: Air Quality Monitoring - Diffusion Tube Results
short_description: Air pollution is monitored by the London boroughs within their own area. There are a number of different monitoring methods available, including passive...
long_description: Air pollution is monitored by the London boroughs within their own area. There are a number of different monitoring methods available, including passive diffusion tubes. These diffusion tubes are small, passive samplers which measure the concentration of nitrogen dioxide (NO2) in the air at a certain location, providing a monthly average measurement. The diffusion tubes supplement the existing network of more accurate reference analysers across London. London boroughs collate and report their diffusion tube monitoring data to the GLA and individually publish the results on their own websites. title: Data Centres Utilisation
short_description: This dataset shows the maximum observed utilisations of operational data centres identified in UK Power Networks' region. The utilisations have been...
long_description: This dataset shows the maximum observed utilisations of operational data centres identified in UK Power Networks' region. The utilisations have been determined using actual demand data from connected sites within UK Power Networks licence areas, from 1 January 2023 onwards. Maximum utilisations are expressed proportionally, by comparing the maximum half-hourly observed import power seen across the site's meter point(s), against the meter's maximum import capacity. Units for both measures are apparent power, in kilovolt amperes (kVA). To protect the identity of the sites, data points have been anonymised and only the site's voltage level information has been provided - and our estimation of the data centre type - has been provided.

long_description: The Flood Zones show the probability of river and sea flooding, ignoring the presence of defences. There are four zones, 1, 2, 3a and 3b, that reflect the annual probability of flooding happening. This map shows all areas with more than a 1 in 1,000 annual probability of either river or sea flooding by combining zones 2, 3a and 3b. If you want to submit a planning application within these zones, you will need to do a flood risk assessment. For a full explanation of when a flood risk assessment is required, visit https://www.gov.uk/guidance/flood-risk-assessment-for-planning-applications#when-you-need-an-assessment. To view more detailed information on the flood zones, visit https://flood-map-for-planning.service.gov.uk/.

short_description: The central London Ultra Low Emission Zone (ULEZ) was introduced to clean up London’s most polluted air. It started on 8 April 2019, operates in the...
long_description: The central London Ultra Low Emission Zone (ULEZ) was introduced to clean up London’s most polluted air. It started on 8 April 2019, operates in the existing central London Congestion Charge Zone, 24 hours a day, every day of the year. Vehicles must meet strict emission standards to drive in the central London ULEZ area, or pay a charge. It replaced the Toxicity Charge (T-Charge) in central London and is in addition to the Congestion Charge. For more information about the central London ULEZ please visit-https://tfl.gov.uk/modes/driving/ultra-low-emission-zone

title: London's Zero Carbon Pathways Tool
short_description: London’s Zero Carbon Pathways Tool shows four ways that carbon emissions might be reduced to net zero by 2030. The Tool, developed by Element Energy,...
long_description: London’s Zero Carbon Pathways Tool shows four ways that carbon emissions might be reduced to net zero by 2030. The Tool, developed by Element Energy, accompanies the report “ Analysis of a Net Zero 2030 Target for Greater London ” also written by Element Energy. This report should be read in conjunction with the tool for an explanation of the assumptions behind the four pathways. In the Tool, energy and carbon results are presented by sector, as well as some sector specific results such as vehicle kilometres for transport and supply by fuel type for district heat. Modelling has been done on a pan-London basis, so borough level results are an estimate only. This Zero Carbon Pathways Tool is an update of an earlier tool published in 2018, which showed pathways to net zero emissions by 2050.

short_description: This study estimates the long-term health impacts of exposure to air pollution in London from 2016 to 2050. The study finds that by 2050 policies in the...
long_description: This study estimates the long-term health impacts of exposure to air pollution in London from 2016 to 2050. The study finds that by 2050 policies in the London Environment Strategy (including the central London Ultra Low Emission Zone and expansion to the North and South circular roads) will result in: almost 300,000 Londoners avoiding new diseases attributable to air pollution, such as coronary heart disease, lung cancer and dementia. This is a reduction of around one in every four air pollution related diseases a cost saving to London’s NHS and social care system of around £5 billion one million fewer air pollution related hospital admissions in London You can read the full report here: https://www.london.gov.uk/what-we-do/environment/pollution-and-air-quality/modelling-long-term-health-

long_description: Two Google Street View cars, equipped with reference-type air quality monitors, are measuring air pollution over approximately 600 eight-hour shifts between autumn 2018 and autumn 2019. The cars use fast-response, research-grade instruments to precisely measure pollution concentrations approximately every 1-10 seconds. Pollutants measured include black carbon (BC), CO₂, NO, NO₂, O3, PM2.5 (and other PM size ranges) on a variety of London roadways. The National Physical Laboratory is responsible for regular checks of instrument performance and periodic calibrations. The cars are collecting data from early morning to late evening, Monday to Friday – providing a representative view of on-road air pollution during these hours. long_description: The Breathe London stationary network is made up of 100 AQMesh pods, each containing a collection of small sensor-based air quality monitors that offer near real-time localised air quality information. In some locations, the pods measure ozone (O3), also using electrochemical sensors. long_description: For an urban heat island map during an average summer see this dataset. A heatwave refers to a prolonged period of unusually hot weather. long_description: For an urban heat island map during a warm summer see this dataset. This map shows the results of an ‘UrbClim’ simulation for the mean temperature at midnight during the summer of 2011 (May to September) at a resolution of 250m. This summer has been selected as a typical summer for a West-European city. On average, the night-time temperature is approximately 4°C higher in the city centre. During some hot nights, even larger effects are observed. UrbClim is an urban climate model designed to model and study the urban heat island effect (UHI) at a spatial resolution of a few hundred meters. long_description: Number of properties in a floodplain and those with a significant risk of flooding. NAFRA - National Flood Risk Assessment. title: Excess Winter Deaths, Borough
short_description: Excess Winter Deaths (EWD) by age and conditions (underlying cause of death) expressed as average per year based on 7 years pooled data, 2004-2011. EWD...
long_description: Excess Winter Deaths (EWD) by age and conditions (underlying cause of death) expressed as average per year based on 7 years pooled data, 2004-2011. EWD trend expressed as average per year based on 3 years data. The Excess Winter Mortality Index (EWM Index was calculated based on the 'ONS Method' which defines the winter period as December to March, and the non-winter period as August to November of that same year and April to July of the following year. This winter period was selected as they are the months which over the last 50 years have displayed above average monthly mortality. However, if mortality starts to increase prior to this, for example in November, the number of deaths in the non-winter period will increase, which in turn will decrease the estimate of excess winter mortality.

long_description: The UK House Price Index (UK HPI) captures changes in the value of residential properties. The UK HPI uses sales data collected on residential housing transactions, whether for cash or with a mortgage. For more information, please read the Land Registry's documentation: https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/about-the-uk-house-price-index/about-the-uk-house-price-index From February 2025, the UK House Price Index has been re-referenced from January 2015 to January 2023. Historical data from 1995 onwards has also been revised to align with this update. These changes reflect market shifts, including an increase in sales of smaller properties and fewer sales in higher-value areas like London. For more details, see the UK HPI publication for more information - https://www.gov.uk/government

long_description: Housing Research Notes are a series of analytical reports from the Greater London Authority focusing on individual issues of relevance to housing policy in London. The most recent Housing Research Note (published in November 2023) estimates the annual cost to the NHS of homes in poor condition in London. It also estimates the cost of repairing all the homes in London that are in poor condition, calculating how long it would take the savings to pay off the repair costs. The analysis is broken down by tenure and compared with the same figures for the rest of England. Previous Housing Research Notes have analysed topics including housing supply, Help to Buy policy, short-term lettings, international comparisons, the factors behind increasing private rents and race equality.

Council tax can be measured in ‘Band D’ or in ‘per dwelling’ terms. Band D has historically been used as the standard for comparing council tax levels between and across local authorities, as this measure is not affected by the varying distribution of properties in bands that can be found across authorities. Additionally, the number of Band D equivalent dwellings are collected at around the same time that the council tax levels are set by the local authority, whereas the ‘per dwelling’ calculation uses chargeable dwellings figures that are captured some five months in advance of setting council tax levels. title: Daily Areal Rainfall Calculations
short_description: Areal rainfall is calculated at a daily timestep for 5 areal units which cover London using the Soil Moisture Model developed and run by the Environment...
long_description: Areal rainfall is calculated at a daily timestep for 5 areal units which cover London using the Soil Moisture Model developed and run by the Environment Agency. For each areal unit, the areal rainfall and soil moisture deficits (SMDs) are calculated. Areal rainfall – This is calculated using rainfall data collected at one or more rain gauges situated within each areal unit. This is then averaged and weighted across each areal unit. Soil moisture deficits (SMDs) are calculated using the areal rainfall data and the potential evaporation (PE) estimated for each unit.Realistically, the SMD would vary slightly during the day given showers/thunderstorms etc and the PE would also vary with the time of day and temperature.This sort of detail is not available and unnecessary for most water resource

title: Cycling Infrastructure Database
short_description: London’s Cycling Infrastructure Database (CID) is the world’s largest and most comprehensive database of cycling infrastructure, containing comprehensive...
long_description: London’s Cycling Infrastructure Database (CID) is the world’s largest and most comprehensive database of cycling infrastructure, containing comprehensive details of cycling infrastructure in the Capital. The CID is intended to address barriers to cycling by providing Londoners with clear and accurate information about cycling infrastructure, helping them plan cycle journeys with confidence. The CID is a core part of our Cycling Action Plan , which sets out how TfL, boroughs and others will work together to make London the world’s best big city for cycling. To create the database, TfL have surveyed every street in every London borough to collect information on over 240,000 pieces of infrastructure, covering an area of 1,595 square kilometres. long_description: A two-part report commissioned by the GLA in 2017 as part of the review of the London Plan. It provides a photographic record of the views from each of the 61 Assessment Points identified by the London Views Management Framework (LVMF) The report is divided into two volumes to assist ease of handling. The two volumes deal with different categories of views as follows: Part 1: River Prospects (designated views 10-22) Part 2: London Panoramas (designated views 1-6) Linear Views (designated views 7-9) Townscape Views (designated views 23-27) On the first page of the report for each Assessment Point, brief descriptive comments are provided to highlight relevant issues concerning each view.

long_description: This dataset will no longer be updated. Cycle flows on the Transport for London Road Network (TLRN). The purpose of this indicator is to assess the level of cycle use on the TLRN. The overall ambition is to increase cycling levels by 400% such that by 2025 cycling will equate to a 5% mode share of all journey trips. This indicator does not represent cycling across London as a whole, It only represents cycling on the 5% of London’s roads that are the TLRN. The indicator is presented as an indexed flow relative to a baseline of March 2000 (a flow level that is represented as 100 on the index). Definition: Sixty automatic cycle counters on the TLRN provide sample counts of cyclists using the network. long_description: Since 2014 the GLA’s Regeneration Unit have been developing and delivering an innovative and pioneering new tool to support local investment in London’s communities: The Mayor’s Crowdfunding Programme. The initiative explores the potential for the GLA to pledge to civic crowdfunding projects as ‘one of the crowd’, enabling London’s citizens to deliver and manage spaces and places that suit local needs and identities. title: Alternative Vote referendum results, Borough
short_description: The alternative vote (AV) referendum was a public vote held across the United Kingdom on 5 May 2011 in which the electorate voted on a proposal to introduce...
long_description: The alternative vote (AV) referendum was a public vote held across the United Kingdom on 5 May 2011 in which the electorate voted on a proposal to introduce a new voting system. The question posed by the referendum was "At present, the UK uses the "first past the post" system to elect MPs to the House of Commons. Should the "alternative vote" system be used instead?" The overall result for the UK was clear, with around 68 per cent of the population voting ‘No’ to AV. In London the result was closer with 60 per cent voting ‘No’, and the capital accounted for most of the areas in the UK that returned a 'Yes' majority. Results are analysed in DMAG Briefing 2011-04, and a spreadsheet of the full results is available. Data and reports for other Elections.

long_description: The Rolling O&D survey (RODS) is a rolling programme to capture information about journeys on the LUL network. Warning: It is important to note that these data are reconciled to November counts and represent the number of people travelling on a typical (or average) weekday. Therefore, year-on-year RODS fluctuations do not necessarily reflect whole-year annual demand changes. Furthermore, these data are adjusted to remove the effect of any abnormal circumstances that may effect demand such as industrial action or long-term closures. long_description: Transport for London's (TFL) Public Transport Accessibility Levels (PTALs) PTALS are a detailed and accurate measure of the accessibility of a point to the public transport network, taking into account walk access time and service availability. The method is essentially a way of measuring the density of the public transport network at any location within Greater London. Each area is graded between 0 and 6b, where a score of 0 is very poor access to public transport, and 6b is excellent access to public transport. The current methodology was developed in 1992, by the London Borough of Hammersmith and Fulham. title: TfL Live Traffic Cameras
short_description: The live traffic camera feed provides images from 177 cameras at key sites across the Capital, showing what's happening on London's streets. All images are...
long_description: The live traffic camera feed provides images from 177 cameras at key sites across the Capital, showing what's happening on London's streets. All images are TfL branded, have a location description, and date and time-stamp. They are refreshed at least every three minutes. short_description: Historical weather station observations from 1853 to present across the UK Met Office surface observation network.
long_description: MIDAS Open provides daily and hourly observations from the UK land surface station network, including temperature, rainfall, wind speed, sunshine, humidity and visibility. Available via CEDA (Centre for Environmental Data Analysis). Covers over 5,000 station-years of data. London stations include Heathrow, Kew Gardens, St James's Park and Hampstead. Essential for climate and weather analysis.
schema_fields: src_id|integer|Station identifier;ob_end_time|datetime|Observation end time;air_temperature|float|Air temp °C;wind_speed|float|Mean wind speed m/s;precipitation|float|Precipitation mm
notes: CEDA registration required. London stations: Heathrow (708), Kew (696), St James's Park.

title: HadUK-Grid 1km Gridded Climate Observations
short_description: 1km gridded daily and monthly UK climate data for temperature, rainfall, sunshine and more from 1836 to present.
long_description: HadUK-Grid is a gridded dataset of UK climate variables derived from the Met Office MIDAS station network. Provides 1km resolution daily and monthly grids for: maximum and minimum temperature, mean temperature, precipitation, sunshine, mean wind speed, mean sea level pressure, relative humidity and snow lying. Full UK coverage. Essential for spatial climate analysis and impact modelling.
schema_fields: latitude|float|Grid cell latitude;longitude|float|Grid cell longitude;tasmax|float|Daily maximum temperature °C;tasmin|float|Daily minimum temperature °C;pr|float|Precipitation mm/day
notes: FORMAT: NetCDF — requires specialist software (Python xarray, QGIS). CEDA registration required.

title: DEFRA UK Air Quality Measurements
short_description: Hourly air quality pollutant measurements from the Automatic Urban and Rural Network (AURN) across the UK.
long_description: The Automatic Urban and Rural Network (AURN) operated by DEFRA and agencies provides continuous automated measurements of air quality pollutants from ~300 monitoring stations. Covers NO2, PM2.5, PM10, O3, SO2, CO. London has the densest network in the UK with central London stations (Marylebone Road, Bloomsbury, Eltham). Data available via the UK-AIR website and API.
provider: Department for Environment, Food & Rural Affairs (DEFRA)
schema_fields: site_id|string|Station code;date_time|datetime|Measurement datetime;no2_ugm3|float|NO2 μg/m³;pm25_ugm3|float|PM2.5 μg/m³;pm10_ugm3|float|PM10 μg/m³
notes: London sites include: MY1 (Marylebone Road), BL0 (Bloomsbury), ELT (Eltham).

title: London Gazette Insolvency and Company Notices API
short_description: Structured XML/JSON feed of all statutory insolvency, company and personal notices published in the London Gazette since 1665.
long_description: The London Gazette publishes statutory notices as required by UK law. The Gazette Linked Data API provides programmatic access to all notice types including compulsory liquidations, administrations, CVAs, bankruptcy orders, company dissolution notices, capital reduction notices and public appointments. Notices available as JSON, XML, Atom feed and CSV. Approximately 300,000+ notices per year.
access_method: Free API — no registration for basic access
schema_fields: notice_id|string|Gazette notice identifier;notice_type|string|Insolvency/Company/Personal;entity_name|string|Subject company or person;issue_date|date|Publication date;content|text|Full notice text
sample_rows: 3456789|Winding-Up Order|Acme Trading Ltd|2024-12-15|The Court made a Winding-up Order...
notes: Longitudinal datasets (linked entity data across multiple notices) available under commercial licence.

title: SRA Solicitors Register
short_description: Searchable register of all SRA-regulated solicitors, law firms and other legal service providers in England and Wales.
long_description: The Solicitors Regulation Authority maintains a public register of all regulated solicitors (160,000+), law firms, licensed bodies and approved regulators. Data includes solicitor name, firm name, town, authorisation status, areas of law and any regulatory history. Available via web search and bulk download. High value for legal market analysis and client due diligence.
access_method: Free web search; bulk download available
schema_fields: id|string|SRA ID number;first_name|string|First name;last_name|string|Last name;firm_name|string|Employer firm;status|string|Authorised/Suspended/Struck off
sample_rows: 12345|John|Smith|Clifford Chance LLP|Authorised

title: GMC List of Registered Medical Practitioners
short_description: Complete register of all licensed doctors in the UK with specialty, registration status and revalidation information.
long_description: The General Medical Council maintains the List of Registered Medical Practitioners (LRMP) containing all doctors licensed to practise in the UK. Approximately 350,000+ records. Available as a downloadable dataset with name, GMC reference number, registration date, primary qualification, specialty and registration status. Updated daily. The Data Explorer tool allows custom queries.
access_method: Free search; bulk export via Data Explorer
schema_fields: gmc_number|string|GMC reference number;given_name|string|First name;family_name|string|Surname;specialty|string|Primary specialty;status|string|Registered/Suspended/Erased
sample_rows: 1234567|Jane|Doe|General Practice|Registered
notes: Full register bulk download may require formal request to GMC.

title: NMC Register of Nurses and Midwives
short_description: Annual de-identified dataset of all registered nurses, midwives and nursing associates in the UK.
long_description: The Nursing and Midwifery Council publishes an annual open dataset of all registered nurses, midwives and nursing associates in the UK. Over 860,000 registrants. The open data version is de-identified but includes registration type, year of first registration, country of training, field of practice (adult/child/mental health/learning disability) and employment sector. Real-time register searchable on website.
schema_fields: registration_type|string|Nurse/Midwife/Nursing Associate;year_first_registered|integer|Year of initial registration;country_of_training|string|Country where trained;field_of_practice|string|Adult/Child/Mental Health/LD;employment_sector|string|NHS/Independent/Education
sample_rows: Nurse|2018|England|Adult|NHS

title: ARB Register of Architects
short_description: Statutory register of all 42,000+ architects in the UK — the only legal route to the title of "architect".
long_description: The Architects Registration Board maintains the statutory register of all individuals legally entitled to use the title "architect" in the UK. ~42,000 registrants. Download contains name, registration number, registered address (business or personal), registration type (Part 1/2/3) and qualifications. Annual bulk download available.
schema_fields: registration_number|string|ARB registration number;first_name|string|First name;last_name|string|Surname;address_town|string|Town/city of practice;qualification|string|Primary qualification
sample_rows: 083007D|James|Foster|London|B.Arch

title: EPC Energy Performance Certificates Bulk Data
short_description: Energy efficiency ratings for 30+ million domestic and non-domestic properties in England and Wales.
long_description: The MHCLG publishes bulk download access to all Energy Performance Certificates (EPCs) lodged since 2008 for domestic and non-domestic properties. Over 30 million records. Each certificate includes property address (with UPRN), energy efficiency rating (A-G), potential rating, property type, floor area, heating type, and environmental impact rating. Updated daily. Comprehensive London coverage.
provider: Ministry of Housing, Communities & Local Government (MHCLG)
schema_fields: uprn|integer|Unique Property Reference Number;address|string|Full property address;postcode|string|Postcode;current_energy_rating|string|A-G rating;property_type|string|House/Flat/Bungalow etc;total_floor_area|float|Floor area m²
sample_rows: 100023336956|15 High Street|EC1A 1BB|C|Flat|65.0
notes: Highly valuable for housing, climate and property use cases. 30M records — requires bulk download split by local authority.

title: Bank of England Bankstats
short_description: Monthly statistical tables on UK monetary and financial statistics including money supply, lending and interest rates.
long_description: The Bank of England publishes Bankstats — a comprehensive collection of monthly statistical tables on UK monetary and financial sector activity. Covers: monetary aggregates (M4), bank lending to households and businesses, consumer credit, mortgage approvals, sterling interest rates, exchange rates and bank balance sheets. Time series back to 1963 for some indicators.
schema_fields: series_code|string|BoE series identifier;description|string|Series title;date|date|Reference month;value|float|Statistical value;units|string|£ millions/% etc
sample_rows: LPMVWYR|M4 lending to private sector|2024-12|3456789|£ millions

title: Bank of England Interactive Statistical Database
short_description: Interactive database of 50,000+ Bank of England statistical series covering banking, financial markets and the economy.
long_description: The BoE Statistical Interactive Database (IADB) provides access to 50,000+ statistical time series covering UK monetary, banking, financial market and economic statistics. Topics include interest rates, exchange rates, equity prices, gilt yields, bank lending, consumer credit, monetary aggregates and balance of payments. API access available for programmatic queries.
schema_fields: series_code|string|Series identifier;series_name|string|Description;date|date|Observation date;value|float|Observation value
sample_rows: IUDBEDR|Bank Rate|2024-12-31|4.75
tags: bank of england,interest rates,gilt yields,exchange rates,financial markets,api,statistics

title: VOA Non-Domestic Rating Valuations (Business Rates)
short_description: Rateable values and property details for all non-domestic (commercial) properties in England.
long_description: The Valuation Office Agency publishes non-domestic rating valuation data for all commercial properties in England subject to business rates. Data includes property address (with UARN), description, rateable value, assessment basis and measurements. Updated at each revaluation (2017 and 2023 lists available). Over 2 million commercial properties including the full London commercial property stock.
schema_fields: uarn|string|Unique Assessment Reference Number;firm_name|string|Occupier name;property_address|string|Property address;scat_code|string|Special Category code (property type);rateable_value|integer|Rateable value £
sample_rows: 1234567890|Tesco Stores Ltd|High Street London EC1A 1BB|249|450000
notes: High commercial value for retail, property and economic analysis.

title: VOA Council Tax Bands
short_description: Council tax band (A-H) for every residential property in England, with address and local authority.
long_description: The Valuation Office Agency publishes council tax banding data for all residential properties in England. Over 26 million properties with band (A-H), full address, local authority code and billing authority. Useful for housing valuation analysis, linking to EPC and price paid data, and understanding residential property distribution across London boroughs.
schema_fields: ba_code|string|Billing authority code;property_id|string|Property identifier;address|string|Full address;postcode|string|Postcode;band|string|Council tax band A-H
sample_rows: E09000019|12345678|1 High Street London|EC1A 1BB|E

title: DfT Road Traffic Statistics
short_description: Annual and monthly vehicle counts on all A-roads and motorways in Great Britain, with local road estimates.
long_description: The Department for Transport publishes road traffic statistics covering vehicle counts from the permanent automatic traffic counters (ATC) network and manual counts. Data available at link level for major roads, and local authority aggregate estimates for minor roads. Covers all vehicle types (cars, LGVs, HGVs, motorcycles, pedal cycles). Annual major road traffic data extends back to 1993.
record_count: Millions of count records by year and road
schema_fields: count_point_id|string|Count point reference;local_authority_name|string|Local authority;road_name|string|Road name or number;year|integer|Reference year;aadf|integer|Annual average daily flow (vehicles)

title: ONS Nomis Local Area Labour Market Statistics
short_description: Detailed labour market statistics for every UK local authority including London boroughs from the LFS, APS and BRES.
long_description: Nomis is the ONS official Labour Market Statistics portal providing custom extracts of Annual Population Survey (APS), Labour Force Survey (LFS), Business Register and Employment Survey (BRES), Claimant Count and Census data. Allows extraction at ward, MSOA, local authority and constituency level. Critical for borough-level economic analysis with quarterly updates for most series.
provider: Office for National Statistics via Nomis
geographic_coverage: United Kingdom; ward and local authority level
access_method: Free download and API — registration recommended
schema_fields: area_code|string|ONS geography code;area_name|string|Area name;date|date|Reference period;variable|string|Indicator name;value|float|Statistical value
sample_rows: E09000013|Hammersmith and Fulham|2024-Q3|Employment rate|79.2

title: Electoral Commission Political Donations and Campaign Finance
short_description: Searchable register of all donations, loans and regulated transactions to UK political parties and candidates.
long_description: The Electoral Commission publishes all reported donations above £7,500 (national threshold) and £1,500 (local threshold) to UK political parties, MPs, candidates and other regulated donees. Includes donor name, type, amount, date and recipient. Also covers campaign spending returns. London-based donors and GLA candidates available. Data from 2001.
record_count: Hundreds of thousands of reported transactions
schema_fields: entity_name|string|Recipient party or candidate;donor_name|string|Donor name;donor_type|string|Individual/Company/Trade Union;amount|float|Donation amount £;date_accepted|date|Date donation accepted
sample_rows: Conservative Party|Acme Holdings Ltd|Company|50000|2024-09-15

title: Planning London Datahub
short_description: Real-time planning application tracking across all 35 London planning authorities from 2004 to present.
long_description: The Planning London Datahub (PLH) is the GLA's centralised system collecting planning application data from all 33 London boroughs and the City of London. Contains every planning application submitted from 2004 with decision dates, application type, development category, description and location. Includes strategic applications determined by the Mayor of London. Updated in real-time from borough systems.
schema_fields: application_id|string|Unique application reference;borough|string|London borough;application_type|string|Full/Outline/LDC etc;decision|string|Permitted/Refused/Pending;development_description|string|Description of proposed development
sample_rows: 2024/1234|Tower Hamlets|Full|Permitted|Demolition of existing building and construction of 45 residential units
notes: Contact GLA for API access. High priority for property development, housing supply and planning analysis.

title: National Rail Darwin Live Departure Boards
short_description: Real-time train running information API for all National Rail stations including arrivals, departures, delays and cancellations.
long_description: Darwin is National Rail's data feeds platform providing push data streams and web service APIs for real-time train movement information across the national rail network. Includes live departure boards, train service details, delay attribution and disruption messaging. Covers all London terminal stations (Paddington, Victoria, Liverpool Street, Waterloo, etc.) and commuter routes.
licence: Commercial — licence required (free tier for developers)
format: JSON REST API, XML push feeds
access_method: Free developer tier; commercial licence for production
schema_fields: crs|string|3-letter station CRS code;scheduledDeparture|datetime|Scheduled departure time;estimatedDeparture|datetime|Estimated actual departure;platform|string|Departure platform;destination|string|Final destination name
notes: Developer access free; production use requires commercial licence. Essential for commuter intelligence.

title: DVLA Vehicle Licensing Statistics
short_description: Annual and quarterly statistics on all licensed vehicles in Great Britain by type, fuel type and geography.
long_description: DVLA publishes detailed vehicle licensing statistics for all registered vehicles in Great Britain. Includes breakdowns by vehicle type (cars, motorcycles, HGVs, buses), fuel type (petrol, diesel, electric, hybrid), body style, age and local authority. Strong London data with borough-level electric vehicle penetration metrics. Data from 1994.
schema_fields: local_authority_code|string|ONS area code;local_authority_name|string|Area name;quarter|date|Reference quarter;body_type|string|Vehicle body type;fuel_type|string|Petrol/Diesel/Battery Electric/Hybrid;count|integer|Number of licensed vehicles
sample_rows: E09000013|Hammersmith and Fulham|2024-Q3|Cars|Battery Electric|4590
notes: EV penetration data by borough is highly valuable for climate and transport planning.

title: Camden Open Data — Planning Applications
short_description: Every planning application submitted to London Borough of Camden since 2010 with decision outcomes.
long_description: Camden Council publishes all planning applications submitted since 2010 via its Open Data portal. Each record includes application reference, address, description, applicant, application type, decision (granted/refused/pending), decision date and planning officer. Updated weekly. ~5,000 applications per year. Exemplar of borough-level planning transparency.
schema_fields: case_reference|string|Application reference;site_address|string|Property address;development_type|string|Application type;decision|string|Granted/Refused/Pending;decision_date|date|Decision date
sample_rows: 2024/1234/P|15 High Street Camden NW1|Full Application|Granted|2024-11-20
notes: API available via Socrata SODA. Model dataset for London borough planning data.

title: Barnet Open Data — 450+ Borough Datasets
short_description: 450+ datasets from the London Borough of Barnet covering planning, housing, environment, finance and licensing.
long_description: Open Barnet (open.barnet.gov.uk) is one of London's most comprehensive borough open data portals with over 450 datasets. Key datasets include: business rates addresses, HMO licensing register, FOI requests archive (5 years), tree preservation orders, parking PCN dashboard, schools data, council expenditure and licensed premises. API-enabled.
schema_fields: UNKNOWN — varies by individual dataset
sample_rows: UNKNOWN — varies by individual dataset
notes: FORMAT: Multiple — umbrella entry for Barnet portal. Engineer should catalogue individual datasets separately.

short_description: Open data portal for London Borough of Redbridge covering environment, finance, housing, education and roads.
long_description: Redbridge DataShare (data.redbridge.gov.uk) provides open datasets from the London Borough of Redbridge across categories including council information, environment, finance, democracy, education, property, housing and vehicles/roads. API enabled with JSON and XML formats. Datasets include street scene incidents, spending data, member allowances and roads/footpaths.

sample_rows: UNKNOWN — varies by individual dataset

long_description: data.southwark.gov.uk provides open datasets from Southwark Council. Key areas: Population, Children & Young People, Health & Social Care, Environment, Housing, Community Safety, Deprivation, Economy & Employment. Notable datasets include social housing asset data, council expenditure and 360Giving-format grants data.
